members.name: Clear
members.summary: Clears the tree. This removes all items. Prints an error and does not allow clearing the tree if called during mouse selection.

members.name: CreateItem
members.signature: public TreeItem CreateItem(TreeItem parent, int index)
members.summary: Creates an item in the tree and adds it as a child of [param parent], which can be either a valid [TreeItem] or [code]null[/code]. If [param parent] is [code]null[/code], the root item will be the parent, or the new item will be the root itself if the tree is empty. The new item will be the [param index]-th child of parent, or it will be the last child if there are not enough siblings. Prints an error and returns [code]null[/code] if called during mouse selection, or if the [param parent] does not belong to this tree.

members.name: DeselectAll
members.summary: Deselects all tree items (rows and columns). In [constant SELECT_MULTI] mode also removes selection cursor.

members.name: EditSelected
members.summary: Edits the selected tree item as if it was clicked. Either the item must be set editable with [method TreeItem.set_editable] or [param force_edit] must be [code]true[/code]. Returns [code]true[/code] if the item could be edited. Fails if no item is selected.

members.name: EnsureCursorIsVisible
members.summary: Makes the currently focused cell visible. This will scroll the tree if necessary. In [constant SELECT_ROW] mode, this will not do horizontal scrolling, as all the cells in the selected row is focused logically. [b]Note:[/b] Despite the name of this method, the focus cursor itself is only visible in [constant SELECT_MULTI] mode.

members.name: GetButtonIdAtPosition
members.summary: Returns the button ID at [param position], or -1 if no button is there.

members.name: GetColumnAtPosition
members.summary: Returns the column index at [param position], or -1 if no item is there.

members.name: GetColumnExpandRatio
members.summary: Returns the expand ratio assigned to the column.

members.name: GetColumnTitle
members.summary: Returns the column's title.

members.name: GetColumnTitleAlignment
members.summary: Returns the column title alignment.

members.name: GetColumnTitleDirection
members.summary: Returns column title base writing direction.

members.name: GetColumnTitleLanguage
members.summary: Returns column title language code.

members.name: GetColumnTitleTooltipText
members.summary: Returns the column title's tooltip text.

members.name: GetColumnWidth
members.summary: Returns the column's width in pixels.

members.name: GetCustomDrawingCanvasItem
members.summary: Returns the internal canvas item designated for custom drawing. See [method TreeItem.set_custom_draw_callback]. [b]Note:[/b] This canvas item clears automatically on each Tree draw call.

members.name: GetCustomPopupRect
members.summary: Returns the rectangle for custom popups. Helper to create custom cell controls that display a popup. See [method TreeItem.set_cell_mode].

members.name: GetDropSectionAtPosition
members.summary: Returns the drop section at [param position], as permitted by enabled [enum DropModeFlags]. - [code]-1[/code] if the position is [b]above[/b] the item. Typically used to insert as the item's previous sibling. - [code]0[/code] if the position is [b]on[/b] the item. Typically used to insert as the item's last child. - [code]1[/code] if the position is [b]below[/b] the item, when the item has no children. Typically used to insert as the item's next sibling. If the item [i]does[/i] have children, this section is still reachable by hovering to the left of the item's collapse arrow, and below. - [code]2[/code] if the position is [b]below[/b] the item, when the item has children. Typically used to insert as the item's first child. - [code]-100[/code] if the position is not over any item, or no [enum DropModeFlags] are set. See [enum DropModeFlags] for a description of each drop region. To get the item which the returned drop section refers to, use [method get_item_at_position].

members.name: GetEdited
members.summary: Returns the currently edited item. Can be used with [signal item_edited] to get the item that was modified. [codeblocks] [gdscript] func _ready(): $Tree.item_edited.connect(on_Tree_item_edited) func on_Tree_item_edited(): print($Tree.get_edited()) # This item just got edited (e.g. checked). [/gdscript] [csharp] public override void _Ready() { GetNode<Tree>("Tree").ItemEdited += OnTreeItemEdited; } public void OnTreeItemEdited() { GD.Print(GetNode<Tree>("Tree").GetEdited()); // This item just got edited (e.g. checked). } [/csharp] [/codeblocks]

members.name: GetEditedColumn
members.summary: Returns the column for the currently edited item.

members.name: GetItemAreaRect
members.signature: public Rect2 GetItemAreaRect(TreeItem item, int column, int buttonIndex)
members.summary: Returns the rectangle area for the specified [TreeItem]. If [param column] is specified, only get the position and size of that column, otherwise get the rectangle containing all columns. If a button index is specified, the rectangle of that button will be returned.

members.name: GetItemAtPosition
members.summary: Returns the tree item at the specified position (relative to the tree origin position).

members.name: GetNextSelected
members.summary: Returns the next selected [TreeItem] after the given one, or [code]null[/code] if the end is reached. If [param from] is [code]null[/code], this returns the first selected item.

members.name: GetPressedButton
members.summary: Returns the last pressed button's index.

members.name: GetRoot
members.summary: Returns the tree's root item, or [code]null[/code] if the tree is empty.

members.name: GetScroll
members.summary: Returns the current scrolling position.

members.name: GetSelected
members.summary: Returns the currently focused item, or [code]null[/code] if no item is focused. In [constant SELECT_ROW] and [constant SELECT_SINGLE] modes, the focused item is same as the selected item. In [constant SELECT_MULTI] mode, the focused item is the item under the focus cursor, not necessarily selected. To get the currently selected item(s), use [method get_next_selected].

members.name: GetSelectedColumn
members.summary: Returns the currently focused column, or -1 if no column is focused. In [constant SELECT_SINGLE] mode, the focused column is the selected column. In [constant SELECT_ROW] mode, the focused column is always 0 if any item is selected. In [constant SELECT_MULTI] mode, the focused column is the column under the focus cursor, and there are not necessarily any column selected. To tell whether a column of an item is selected, use [method TreeItem.is_selected].

members.name: IsColumnClippingContent
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the column has enabled clipping (see [method set_column_clip_content]).

members.name: IsColumnExpanding
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the column has enabled expanding (see [method set_column_expand]).

members.name: ScrollToItem
members.signature: public void ScrollToItem(TreeItem item, bool centerOnItem)
members.summary: Causes the [Tree] to jump to the specified [TreeItem].

members.name: SetColumnClipContent
members.signature: public void SetColumnClipContent(int column, bool enable)
members.summary: Allows to enable clipping for column's content, making the content size ignored.

members.name: SetColumnCustomMinimumWidth
members.signature: public void SetColumnCustomMinimumWidth(int column, int minWidth)
members.summary: Overrides the calculated minimum width of a column. It can be set to [code]0[/code] to restore the default behavior. Columns that have the "Expand" flag will use their "min_width" in a similar fashion to [member Control.size_flags_stretch_ratio].

members.name: SetColumnExpand
members.signature: public void SetColumnExpand(int column, bool expand)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the column will have the "Expand" flag of [Control]. Columns that have the "Expand" flag will use their expand ratio in a similar fashion to [member Control.size_flags_stretch_ratio] (see [method set_column_expand_ratio]).

members.name: SetColumnExpandRatio
members.signature: public void SetColumnExpandRatio(int column, int ratio)
members.summary: Sets the relative expand ratio for a column. See [method set_column_expand].

members.name: SetColumnTitle
members.signature: public void SetColumnTitle(int column, String title)
members.summary: Sets the title of a column.

members.name: SetColumnTitleAlignment
members.signature: public void SetColumnTitleAlignment(int column, int titleAlignment)
members.summary: Sets the column title alignment. Note that [constant @GlobalScope.HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_FILL] is not supported for column titles.

members.name: SetColumnTitleDirection
members.signature: public void SetColumnTitleDirection(int column, int direction)
members.summary: Sets column title base writing direction.

members.name: SetColumnTitleLanguage
members.signature: public void SetColumnTitleLanguage(int column, String language)
members.summary: Sets the language code of the given [param column]'s title to [param language]. This is used for line-breaking and text shaping algorithms. If [param language] is empty, the current locale is used.

members.name: SetColumnTitleTooltipText
members.signature: public void SetColumnTitleTooltipText(int column, String tooltipText)
members.summary: Sets the column title's tooltip text.

members.name: SetSelected
members.signature: public void SetSelected(TreeItem item, int column)
members.summary: Selects the specified [TreeItem] and column.

members.name: AllowReselect
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the currently selected cell may be selected again.

members.name: AllowRmbSelect
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], a right mouse button click can select items.

members.name: AllowSearch
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], allows navigating the [Tree] with letter keys through incremental search.

members.name: AutoTooltip
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], tree items with no tooltip assigned display their text as their tooltip. See also [method TreeItem.get_tooltip_text] and [method TreeItem.get_button_tooltip_text].

members.name: ColumnTitlesVisible
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], column titles are visible.

members.name: Columns
members.summary: The number of columns. Prints an error and does not allow setting the columns during mouse selection.

members.name: DropModeFlags
members.summary: The drop mode as an OR combination of flags. See [enum DropModeFlags] constants. Once dropping is done, reverts to [constant DROP_MODE_DISABLED]. Setting this during [method Control._can_drop_data] is recommended. This controls the drop sections, i.e. the decision and drawing of possible drop locations based on the mouse position.

members.name: EnableDragUnfolding
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], tree items will unfold when hovered over during a drag-and-drop. The delay for when this happens is dictated by [theme_item dragging_unfold_wait_msec].

members.name: EnableRecursiveFolding
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], recursive folding is enabled for this [Tree]. Holding down [kbd]Shift[/kbd] while clicking the fold arrow or using [code]ui_right[/code]/[code]ui_left[/code] shortcuts collapses or uncollapses the [TreeItem] and all its descendants.

members.name: HideFolding
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the folding arrow is hidden.

members.name: HideRoot
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the tree's root is hidden.

members.name: ScrollHintMode
members.summary: The way which scroll hints (indicators that show that the content can still be scrolled in a certain direction) will be shown.

members.name: ScrollHorizontalEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables horizontal scrolling.

members.name: ScrollVerticalEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables vertical scrolling.

members.name: SelectMode
members.summary: Allows single or multiple selection. See the [enum SelectMode] constants.

members.name: TileScrollHint
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the scroll hint texture will be tiled instead of stretched. See [member scroll_hint_mode].

signals.name: ButtonClicked
signals.summary: Emitted when a button on the tree was pressed (see [method TreeItem.add_button]).

signals.name: CellSelected
signals.summary: Emitted when a cell is selected.

signals.name: CheckPropagatedToItem
signals.summary: Emitted when [method TreeItem.propagate_check] is called. Connect to this signal to process the items that are affected when [method TreeItem.propagate_check] is invoked. The order that the items affected will be processed is as follows: the item that invoked the method, children of that item, and finally parents of that item.

signals.name: ColumnTitleClicked
signals.summary: Emitted when a column's title is clicked with either [constant MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT] or [constant MOUSE_BUTTON_RIGHT].

signals.name: CustomItemClicked
signals.summary: Emitted when an item with [constant TreeItem.CELL_MODE_CUSTOM] is clicked with a mouse button.

signals.name: CustomPopupEdited
signals.summary: Emitted when a cell with the [constant TreeItem.CELL_MODE_CUSTOM] is clicked to be edited.

signals.name: EmptyClicked
signals.summary: Emitted when a mouse button is clicked in the empty space of the tree.

signals.name: ItemActivated
signals.summary: Emitted when an item is double-clicked, or selected with a [code]ui_accept[/code] input event (e.g. using [kbd]Enter[/kbd] or [kbd]Space[/kbd] on the keyboard).

signals.name: ItemCollapsed
signals.summary: Emitted when an item is expanded or collapsed by clicking on the folding arrow or through code. [b]Note:[/b] Despite its name, this signal is also emitted when an item is expanded.

signals.name: ItemEdited
signals.summary: Emitted when an item is edited.

signals.name: ItemIconDoubleClicked
signals.summary: Emitted when an item's icon is double-clicked. For a signal that emits when any part of the item is double-clicked, see [signal item_activated].

signals.name: ItemMouseSelected
signals.summary: Emitted when an item is selected with a mouse button.

signals.name: ItemSelected
signals.summary: Emitted when an item is selected.

signals.name: MultiSelected
signals.summary: Emitted instead of [signal item_selected] if [member select_mode] is set to [constant SELECT_MULTI].

signals.name: NothingSelected
signals.summary: Emitted when a left mouse button click does not select any item.

values.name: DROP_MODE_DISABLED
values.summary: Disables all drop sections. [b]Note:[/b] This is the default flag, it has no effect when combined with other flags.

values.name: DROP_MODE_INBETWEEN
values.summary: Enables "above item" and "below item" drop sections. The "above item" drop section covers the top half of the item, while the "below item" drop section covers the bottom half, and extends downward to the left of any children. When combined with [constant DROP_MODE_ON_ITEM], these drop sections halve in height and stay at the top and bottom respectively.

values.name: DROP_MODE_ON_ITEM
values.summary: Enables the "on item" drop section. This drop section covers the entire item. When combined with [constant DROP_MODE_INBETWEEN], this drop section halves in height and stays centered vertically.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_BOTH
values.summary: Scroll hints will be shown at the top and bottom.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_BOTTOM
values.summary: Only the bottom scroll hint will be shown.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_DISABLED
values.summary: Scroll hints will never be shown.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_TOP
values.summary: Only the top scroll hint will be shown.

values.name: SELECT_MULTI
values.summary: Allows selection of multiple cells at the same time. From the perspective of items, multiple items are allowed to be selected. And there can be multiple columns selected in each selected item. The focus cursor is visible in this mode, the item or column under the cursor is not necessarily selected.

values.name: SELECT_ROW
values.summary: Allows selection of a single row at a time. From the perspective of items, only a single items is allowed to be selected. And all the columns are selected in the selected item. The focus cursor is always hidden in this mode, but it is positioned at the first column of the current selection, making the currently selected item the currently focused item.

values.name: SELECT_SINGLE
values.summary: Allows selection of a single cell at a time. From the perspective of items, only a single item is allowed to be selected. And there is only one column selected in the selected item. The focus cursor is always hidden in this mode, but it is positioned at the current selection, making the currently selected item the currently focused item.

constants.name: SELECT_SINGLE
constants.summary: Allows selection of a single cell at a time. From the perspective of items, only a single item is allowed to be selected. And there is only one column selected in the selected item. The focus cursor is always hidden in this mode, but it is positioned at the current selection, making the currently selected item the currently focused item.

constants.name: SELECT_ROW
constants.summary: Allows selection of a single row at a time. From the perspective of items, only a single items is allowed to be selected. And all the columns are selected in the selected item. The focus cursor is always hidden in this mode, but it is positioned at the first column of the current selection, making the currently selected item the currently focused item.

constants.name: SELECT_MULTI
constants.summary: Allows selection of multiple cells at the same time. From the perspective of items, multiple items are allowed to be selected. And there can be multiple columns selected in each selected item. The focus cursor is visible in this mode, the item or column under the cursor is not necessarily selected.

constants.name: DROP_MODE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Disables all drop sections. [b]Note:[/b] This is the default flag, it has no effect when combined with other flags.

constants.name: DROP_MODE_ON_ITEM
constants.summary: Enables the "on item" drop section. This drop section covers the entire item. When combined with [constant DROP_MODE_INBETWEEN], this drop section halves in height and stays centered vertically.

constants.name: DROP_MODE_INBETWEEN
constants.summary: Enables "above item" and "below item" drop sections. The "above item" drop section covers the top half of the item, while the "below item" drop section covers the bottom half, and extends downward to the left of any children. When combined with [constant DROP_MODE_ON_ITEM], these drop sections halve in height and stay at the top and bottom respectively.

constants.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Scroll hints will never be shown.

constants.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_BOTH
constants.summary: Scroll hints will be shown at the top and bottom.

constants.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_TOP
constants.summary: Only the top scroll hint will be shown.

constants.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_BOTTOM
constants.summary: Only the bottom scroll hint will be shown.
